fault_hypothesis: Line 67 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`now > effective_ttl`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= effective_ttl`), so when `now` exactly equals the TTL boundary the token is incorrectly returned instead of treated as expired.

constraints.intent: At the expiry check, when now equals effective_ttl the condition must be treated as expired (now >= effective_ttl should trigger return None).
After computing effective_ttl, verify that the subsequent comparison uses >= so that now==effective_ttl is treated as expired; the strict > operator is the bug.
Immediately before effective_ttl is used in the comparison, assert that if now equals effective_ttl the branch will correctly return None (which requires >= not >).
In the else branch (token not expired), now must be strictly less than effective_ttl; if now == effective_ttl we should not reach here.
